default_top_level_agent_count_policy.scope: Generated target teams and nontrivial meta-team work plans. The fixed S2T meta-team itself has six internal service agents.
default_top_level_agent_count_policy.below_range_requires: Clear low-risk consolidation rationale showing no independent boundary is lost.
default_top_level_agent_count_policy.above_range_requires: Strong evidence of hard isolation, multiple independent gates, multiple regulated domains, conflicting context boundaries, or separate credential/workspace requirements.
default_top_level_agent_count_policy.above_eight_rule: Discouraged unless the user explicitly needs hard isolation; include a consolidation plan.

reuse_policy.same_version_rule: If a registered s2t-meta manifest has the same skill2team_version, meta_team_contract_version, meta_team_signature, entry_agent_id, and all registered files exist, reuse it.
reuse_policy.missing_rule: If no usable s2t-meta Codex activation exists and meta-team-first is requested, generate the fixed 1.9.2 meta-team package for Codex preflight, register it, and separately verify active-session invocation. If registered activation cannot be confirmed because the current thread did not hot-load newly written agent config, either use real current-session subagents for fixed S2T work orders and record real_session_subagents, or block with a reason.
reuse_policy.mismatch_rule: If an s2t-meta registration exists but the version or signature differs, do not delete it unless the user explicitly requests force regeneration; otherwise block registered meta-team execution and explain the version/signature mismatch. Do not use fallback role-play in Codex meta-team-first.
reuse_policy.force_rule: On explicit force regeneration, unregister only the files listed in the s2t-meta registry manifest or files with matching Skill2Team ownership markers, then register the fixed meta-team package and require either registered smoke-test evidence or real current-session subagent evidence before claiming meta-team-first execution.

agents.main_function: Intake, route selection, delivery selection, execution-path selection, state tracking, and synthesis ownership.
agents.mission: User-facing entry agent for Skill2Team. Own intake, route selection, delivery selection, execution-path selection, state lookup, work-order creation, and final synthesis ownership.
responsibilities: Intake, route selection, delivery selection, execution-path selection, state tracking, and synthesis ownership.

agents.main_function: Source asset inventory, original workflow extraction, local-resource classification, and migration anchors.
agents.mission: Inventory source skills, prompts, tools, scripts, templates, registry manifests, local resources, workflows, and SOPs; extract current workflow stages, stage-internal deliverables, human intervention points, artifacts, failure modes, and migration anchors.
responsibilities: Source asset inventory, original workflow extraction, local-resource classification, and migration anchors.

agents.main_function: Agent Architecture Map, role boundaries, team topology, skill allocation, and agent-count rationale.
agents.mission: Design the Agent Architecture Map separately from workflow orchestration: role topology, accountability, authority, context boundaries, owned/shared/restricted skills, and the rationale for the chosen top-level agent count.
responsibilities: Agent Architecture Map, role boundaries, team topology, skill allocation, and agent-count rationale.

agents.main_function: Workflow Orchestration Map, workflow preservation gate, control-flow rules, handoff contracts, checkpoints, reruns, and resume policy.
agents.mission: Design the Workflow Orchestration Map separately from agent architecture: runtime stages, concrete nodes/edges, stage-internal deliverables, human intervention choices, gates, fan-out/fan-in, terminal boundaries, artifact lineage, and dependency-aware rerun/resume rules.
responsibilities: Workflow Orchestration Map, control-flow rules, handoff contracts, checkpoints, reruns, and resume policy.

agents.main_function: Codex package artifacts, package manifests, runtime instruction materialization, conformance contracts, design-output archive, design-continuation templates, Codex-only package-end templates, and package-readiness guidance.
agents.mission: Generate Codex agent artifacts, registry/readiness manifests, usage guides, invocation contracts, prompt-rewrite rules, design-result archives, design-continuation templates, and Codex-only package-end register/start/use templates.
responsibilities: Codex package artifacts, package manifests, runtime instruction materialization, conformance contracts, design-output archive, design-continuation templates, Codex-only package-end templates, and package-readiness guidance.

agents.main_function: Independent design/package conformance audit, runtime-instruction materialization review, workflow preservation checks, risk checks, count justification checks, and acceptance-gate review.
agents.mission: Independently audit target-team and generated meta-team design/package quality, source-derived runtime constraint preservation, runtime instruction materialization, human intervention retention choices, local-resource allocation, risks, role boundaries, architecture/workflow separation, and agent-count justification before package handoff.
responsibilities: Independently audit design/package conformance and runtime instruction materialization before package handoff.

Block release with reexecution_required=true when source-derived runtime constraints, startup settings, human waits, gates, checkpoints, local resources, or stage-internal deliverables are missing downstream.
Route blocked work back to the producing meta role; do not repair producer artifacts and approve the reviewer's own changes.

model_invocation_policy.api_runner_exception_label: API-run role simulation, not Codex custom-agent execution
model_invocation_policy.meta_team_first_codex_rule: Codex meta-team-first must use real registered s2t-meta agents when the active session can invoke them. If newly installed custom agents are not hot-loaded in the current thread, real current-session subagents may execute the fixed S2T work orders with current_run_fanout_status=real_session_subagents. Fallback role-play is forbidden; if no real activation/fan-out/handoff can be verified, stop with meta-team-first blocked and a concrete reason.

design_continuation_actions_are_prompt_t: codex package/register guidance using OpenAI Codex model service
post_package_actions_are_codex_only_prom: codex registration/use guidance using OpenAI Codex model service
registered entry-agent use after smoke tests
current-session target-team fan-out when registered target-agent types are unavailable
delivery_policy.meta_team_first_blocking_rule: Execution path meta-team-first with target runtime codex is not a delivery shortcut or fallback mode. It requires fixed s2t-meta package generation/reuse and either registered custom-agent activation evidence or real current-session subagent work-order evidence before design/package work continues.

architecture_default.runtime_dependency: none for Skill2Team package metadata; map to a specific multi-agent runner only in an explicit follow-up
architecture_default.node_binding: one durable accountability boundary maps to one agent profile/node; workflow steps do not automatically become agents

design_quality_gate: stage-internal deliverables are preserved or explicitly rewritten
required user input nodes are inventoried and preserved by default
human intervention retention choices are recorded
startup settings required before first migrated stage are collected or blocked
source-derived runtime constraints are inventoried and assigned materialization targets
design/package conformance preflight passes or names reexecute_from
package_release_gate: design-output archive and Codex-only package-end prompt coverage
entry-agent startup welcome page is present
design-package-conformance.contract.json passes or blocks with reexecution_required=true
runtime-instruction-conformance.json proves constraint ids are in generated agent instructions
blocked conformance routes back to the earliest responsible phase instead of downstream patching
design-output.zip is present and includes the design result
direct-skill and meta-team-first target packages use the same artifact contract

runtime_policy.target_team_codex_execution_rule: Generated target-team execution in Codex should use real registered entry/specialist handoffs when generated target-agent types are available. If same-thread hot-load prevents registered handoff, real current-session target subagents may run only with target_run_fanout_status=real_session_target_subagents and registered smoke status pending. If neither real path is available, stop with target-team execution blocked; sequential or single-agent simulation is forbidden under the Codex target-team label.

audit_subjects: runtime instruction materialization of source-derived constraints
independent_meta_team_audit_policy.reexecution_rule: If independent audit blocks, rerun the earliest responsible source-mapper, workflow-orchestrator, architecture-designer, or runtime-adapter phase before package handoff.
